---
title: "Human Rights GLOA 101"
author: "Byunghwan Son <br> George Mason University"
date: " "
#########################################
linkcolor: red
citecolor: blue
urlcolor: blueß
output: 
  xaringan::moon_reader:
    seal: false
    nature:
      ratio: '16:9'
      highlightStyle: github
      mathjax: "default"  
  #    self-contained: TRUE
  chakra: css/libs/remark-latest.min.js
css: ["default", 
      "css/footer.css", 
      "css/color.css", 
      # "css/xaringan-themer.css",
      default-fonts]
lib_dir: css/libs
beforeInit: "css/macros.js"
# seal: false  
#bibliography: /Users/bson3/Dropbox/latex/mybib2.bib
#remotes::install_github('yihui/xaringan', upgrade = TRUE)

---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
# to use FontAwesome
#htmltools::tagList(rmarkdown::html_dependency_font_awesome())
library(fs)
library(fontawesome)
library(vdemdata)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(WDI)
# library(magick)  
library(ggplot2)
knitr::opts_chunk$set(warning = FALSE, message = FALSE, error=F, echo=F)
options(htmltools.preserve.raw = FALSE)
```

class: right, bottom
background-image: url(https://www.nyc.gov/assets/cchr/images/content/NYC-Human-Rights-Street-Signs.png)
background-size: 110%

# .Huge[.yellow[**GLOA 101**]]

---

.full-width-green[

# Overview

]
<br><br>
--

.content-box-red[
## What we’re talking about:

- .Large[concepts of human rights]
- .Large[diﬀerent ideas about human rights]
- .Large[current challenges]

]

--

.content-box-yellow[
## You will be able to:

- .Large[explain the diﬀerent views on human rights]
- .Large[discuss the challenges to global eﬀorts on human rights protection]
]

---

.full-width-brown[
# Human Rights: the idea
]

<br><br>
--

> .Large[
- .black[The .pink[natural], .Blue[universal], and .purple[inalienable] rights to which *all* humans are entitled. .tiny[McCormick (2025), Ch. 10]]
]

--

> .Large[
- .black[Universal Declaration of Human Rights (UDHR)]
]

## - .orange[global] by definition

--

## - natural & universal rights?

---


.full-width-blue[
# Different Views: Ideological Division
]
<br><br>
--

.content-box-yellow[

### Traditional liberal view ( $\approx$  civil liberties; 'negative' rights)

- .Large[free from repression (religion/politics)]
]

.content-box-red[

### Socialist view ($\approx$ civil rights; 'positive' rights)
- .Large[economic, cultural, and social rights: free from inequality, micro-aggression, marginalization, cultural appropriation, health]
]

--

.content-box-blue[
### **Post-colonial view**: right to independence, development, peace
]

---
class: inverse, center, middle
background-image: url(https://raw.githubusercontent.com/textvulture/textvulture.github.io/refs/heads/master/images/night_background3.jpg)
background-size: 110%

# .huge[Human rights are *complicated*.]

---

.full-width-green[

# Are these human rights? What kind?
]
<br><br>
--

.pull-left[

<iframe width="500" height="500" src="https://www.youtube.com/embed/i4dQZIU6p1U?si=N5TjA2iZBFbHHZkF" title="YouTube video player" frameborder="0" allow="accelerometer; autoplay; clipboard-write; encrypted-media; gyroscope; picture-in-picture; web-share" referrerpolicy="strict-origin-when-cross-origin" allowfullscreen></iframe>

]

--

.pull-right[
<iframe width="500" height="500" src="https://www.youtube.com/embed/79oRVfdk9uE?si=wqgxajDvu7GWiKgS" title="YouTube video player" frameborder="0" allow="accelerometer; autoplay; clipboard-write; encrypted-media; gyroscope; picture-in-picture; web-share" referrerpolicy="strict-origin-when-cross-origin" allowfullscreen></iframe>
]


---

.full-width-green[

# Different Views: relativism

]
<br><br>

--

.pull-left[

## .red[Relativists]

- .Large['Who are you to judge?']
- .Large[Human rights are defined **contextually**.]
- .Large[Cross-cultural blaming is unfair.]
- .Large[**AU**: 'ICC imperialism']
  - .large[most targets are in SSA]
  - .large[selective justice]
  - .large[infringement upon sovereignty]

]


--

.pull-right[

## .blue[Universalists]

- .Large[Human rights are universal.]
- .Large[*Liberal Interventionalism* (.blue['R2P'])]
- .Large[UN; .orange[multilateral] interventions]
- .Large[Cultural relativism is just a cover.]
- .Large[Many ICC cases are self-reported in SSA.]

]

---

.full-width-brown[
# Global Efforst for Human Rights (or the lack thereof)
]
<br><br>
--

.pull-left[
## .hl1[UN Comm. on Human Rights (UNCHR)]

- .Large[a political, inter-govt body]
- .Large[review of HR practices]
- .Large[a ‘forum’ elected by ECOSOC]
- .Large[.purple[yearly] review]
- .Large[criticisms]
  - .large[selective enforcement and politicization]
  - .large['problematic' members (e.g., Sudan (Darfur); Zimbabwe; China)]

]

--

.pull-right[
## .hl2[UN Human Rights Council (UNHRC)]

- .Large[replaced UNCHR in 2006; *still*, a political, int-govt body]
- .Large[elected by UN GA]
- .Large[High Commissioner for Human Rights]
- .Large[.red[year-round] meetings]
- .Large['universal periodic review']
- .Large[member suspension (e.g., Libya 2011)]
]

---

.full-width-blue[
# Global Efforst for Human Rights (or the lack thereof)
]
<br>

--

.pull-left[
# Int'l Criminal Tribunals

- .LARGE[temporary, case-by-case]
- .LARGE[Nuremberg / Tokyo (1945)]
- .LARGE[Former Yugoslavia: ICTY (1991)]
- .LARGE[Rwanda: ICTR (1994)]

]

--

.pull-right[

# Humanitarian Intervention

- .LARGE[UN: “responsibility to protect (R2P)” (2005) `r fa("arrow-right")` “Liberal Interventionism”]
- .LARGE[air strikes; no-fly zones; diplomatic interference]
  - .Large[Libya (2011): the only 'uncontroversial' one]
  - .Large[Côte d’Ivoire (2011)]
  - .Large[Kenya (2007-8)]

]

---

.full-width-green[
# International Criminal Court (Rome Statute 1998; 2002)
]

.pull-left[
### - Genocide, crimes against humanity, egregious human rights violations, and war crimes 

### - Cases that occurred .red[after] 07-01-2002 in a .orange[state] that has .purple[ratified] the treaty or that is committed by a .blue[national] of such a state or in cases referred to it by the .green[UN Security Council]

### - 18 judges (9-year term)

### - local immunities ignored, but local .red[jurisdiction] also needs to be given up.
]


.pull-right[
<br><br><br><br><br><br>
![](https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/8/83/ICC_member_states_world_map.png)

]

---

![](https://justiceinconflict.org/wp-content/uploads/2013/05/screen-shot-2013-05-20-at-12-16-46-pm.png)



---

# Let's play with it a little: .tiny[Can ICC .red[convict] this person? (yes or no)]

--

.content-box-blue[
### - Juan, a Russian citizen, led ethnic cleansing against indigenous people in Uruguay and fled to the United States.
]

--

.content-box-red[
### - Sipho, a South African national, committed a war crime while working as a mercernary in Afghanistan, and fled to Mongolia.
]

--

.content-box-green[
### - Agus, an Indonesian national, led a global human trafficking ring operating from Pakistan and was referred to the ICC by the UNSC.
]

--

.small[All names generated by ChatGPT.]

---

class: inverse, center, middle
background-image: url(https://media2.giphy.com/media/v1.Y2lkPTZjMDliOTUyN2NpMjBrbTI5OHlmd3N3eGM0a2JmcGowanR4NnFmN2J4aWFjazJnNiZlcD12MV9naWZzX3NlYXJjaCZjdD1n/lHfxDepSGlzom6f65K/giphy.gif)
background-size: 110%

# .huge[.yellow[So has it been all bad and confusing?]]

---

# Trend: 'negative' rights .tiny[(.blue[free from torture]; .red[free from political killing]; V-dem data)]

```{r echo=FALSE, error=FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE, fig.width=20, fig.height=9, cache=TRUE}


vdem %>%
#  filter(e_regionpol_6C == 6) %>%
  select(year, v2cltort, v2clkill) %>%
  filter(year>1900) %>%
  gather(key="variable", value="value", -year) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = year, y=value)) +
  geom_smooth(aes(color=variable)) +
  geom_point(aes(color=variable), size=2, alpha=1/20) +
  labs(x = "year", 
       y = "Human Rights") +
  scale_color_manual(labels = c("Freedom From Torture", 
                 "Freedom From Political Killing"),
                     values = c("blue", "red")) +
  theme_minimal() +  
  theme(axis.text=element_text(size=30),
        axis.title=element_text(size=35),
        legend.position = "none",
        legend.text=element_text(size=35),
        legend.title = element_blank()) 

```


---

# Trend: 'positive' rights (WDI; V-dem data)

--

.pull-left[

```{r, echo=FALSE, cache=TRUE}

var = c('life' = 'SP.DYN.LE00.IN'
        )

wdi <- WDI(indicator=var,
           start=1960,
           country = 'all',
           end=2020,
           extra=TRUE)


wdi %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = year, y=life)) +
  geom_point(size=0.2, fill='blue', alpha=0.3) + 
  geom_smooth(color='blue') +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 25) + 
  theme(legend.position = "none") +
  labs(y="Life Expectancy")


```

]

--

.pull-right[

```{r, cache=T}

vdem %>%
  #  filter(e_regionpol_6C == 6) %>%
  select(year, v2svinlaut) %>%
  filter(!is.na(v2svinlaut)) %>%
#  group_by(year) %>%
#  summarise(pct_yes = mean(autonomy == "yes", na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = year, y = v2svinlaut)) +
  geom_point(size=0.1, alpha=0.2) +
  geom_smooth(color='red', se=F) +
  labs(x="", y="Auotnomy") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 25)


```


]
